title: M/S. Rishab Enterprises vs Mr.Arhad Jameel on 19 April, 2022
body: M/S. Rishab Enterprises vs Mr.Arhad Jameel on 19 April, 2022 1 O.S.33/2019 IN THE COURT OF THE XXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE AT BANGALORE CITY - CCH NO.23. DATED THIS THE 19 th DAY OF APRIL, 2022. PRESIDING OFFICER PRESENT : Sri.Mohan Prabhu, M.A., L.LM., XXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, BANGALORE. O.S.No.33/2019 PLAINTIFF/S: M/s. Rishab Enterprises, Represented by its authorised, Smt.Shanti Kanunga W/o Goutham Kanunga Office at No. 79, Siddanna Lane, Nagarthpet cross, Bangalore. Represented by her Special Power of Attorney Sri. Goutham Kanunga S/o Nainmal Kanunga Aged about 44 years, R/at No. 79/2, Siddanna Lane, Nagarthpet cross, Bangalore - 560 002. (By Sri.S.K.M Associates) Vs. DEFENDANT/S:1. Mr.Arhad Jameel, 2 O.S.33/2019 Aged about 41 years, Proprietor / Partner & authorised signatory M/s. M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works No. 1347/46, 5th cross, Rashad Nagar, Near Arabic College, Bangalore - 560 045. And also available at Mr. Arshad Jameel, C/o Roop Creation, Shop No.4A, 4B, 4th floor, Laxmi Tower, No.7, Appaji Rao Lane, C.T.Street, Bangalore - 560 002. 2. M/s. Mahi Embroidery Work, Represented by its Proprietor / Partner & Authorised Signatory, Mr.Arshad Jameel, No.728, 3rd cross, Pillanna Garden, Tannery road, Bangalore - 560 045. And also available at Mr.Arshad Jameel, C/o Roop Creation Shop No. 4A, 4B, 4th floor, Laxmi Tower, No.7, Appaji Rao Lane, C.T.Street, Bangalore - 560 002. (By Sri.SMK, Advocate) ***** 3 O.S.33/2019 Date of institution of suit : 01.01.2019 Nature of suit : Money suit Date of commencement of recording of evidence : 10.02.2021 Date on which the judgment was pronounced : 19.04.2022 Duration of the suit :Year/s Month/s Day/s 03 03 18 JUDGMENT 1. This is a suit for recovery of money. 2. The averments of plaint in brief are that the plaintiff is a dealer in stockist House of Embroidery Threads and plaintiff is running a business under the name and style of "Rishab Enterprises". The defendants have also doing business under the name and style of the "M/s Mahi Embroidery Work". The defendants are the customer of plaintiff, defendants have doing a business with plaintiff on credit bases, on that guise, 4 O.S.33/2019 during the beginning of the year 2015 defendants approached the plaintiff and posing to be as a prospective buyer and wanted to buy the materials from the plaintiff on credit basis. During the month April 2016 defendants are succeeded to convinced the plaintiff to part their valuable materials on credit basis and accordingly defendants are started business with the plaintiff since 2015, as per the terms and conditions, the defendants are requested to plaintiff for pay the amount within 30 days from the date of invoice and further in the beginning period defendants are sincerely paid the transaction amount without any delay. After, payment of due earlier invoice amount defendants used to purchase the new materials of Embroidery threads from plaintiff on credit basis, so that plaintiff was agreed to sell the material to defendants and when the transaction continue with the plaintiff defendants have purchased the materials from plaintiff for that Invoices are raised on 28.04.2016 to 06.05.2016. Accordingly, defendants have purchased the 5 O.S.33/2019 materials in total Rs.4,48,890/- materials at that time defendants have requested with plaintiff and assured that due amount would be clear within 30 days from the date of Invoice i.e., Invoice dates, number and amount as follows: Sl.No. Invoice date Invoice Number Invoice Amount (Rs.) 1 28.04.2016 2481 1,75,952.00 2 30.04.2016 2496 1,35,324.00 3 06.05.2016 2744 1,37,614.00 Total 4,48,890.00 3. After lapse of 30 days the plaintiff had approached the defendants and demanded for repayment of due amount. At that time the defendants have requested the plaintiff that he will arrange the amount within one month. Hence the plaintiff trusted on defendants and waited for some more days. After one month plaintiff requested the defendants to clear legal liability. Again defendants are stated one or other reasons and postponed and delayed to pay defendants legal liability and avoided to receive plaintiffs phone call. The defendants 6 O.S.33/2019 with an intention to cheat the plaintiff and misused trust till today the defendants have not cleared the due amount. Having no other alternative the plaintiff has issued legal notice to the defendants through his advocate on 21.11.2017 by speed post to both addressed of the defendants. The legal notice was returned first address of the defendant on 22.11.2017 with shara insufficient address. Another notice sent to the 2nd address of the defendants also returned on 23.11.2017 with shara insufficient address. Another notice sent to 2nd address of the defendants also returned on 23.11.2017 with shara intimation delivered. Even after receipt of the plaintiffs legal notice the defendants did not reply the notice nor paid the invoice due amount to the plaintiff. Thereafter having no other alternative plaintiff was approached the Ulsoor gate police station on 21.1.2018. After few days the defendants was called to plaintiff and requested the plaintiff to give one month time to make arrangement to pay the invoice due amount. 7 O.S.33/2019 4. After a month the plaintiff was demanded the defendants to clear legal liability then the defendants out of Rs.4,48,890/- paid only Rs.50,000/- on 21.8.2018 to the plaintiff, then the defendants requested the plaintiff to give one more month to clear the remaining amount of Rs.3,98,890/-. The plaintiff was trusted on the defendants words and waited for some more days. After one month the plaintiff requested the defendants to clear legal liability but the defendants postponed and delayed to pay the amount. The defendants again avoided receiving plaintiffs phone call. Thereafter plaintiff was again approached to Ulsoor gate police but the police have not taken any action. Hence the plaintiff has filed suit for recovery of Rs.3,98,890/- along with interest at the rate of 24% per annum. Hence on these grounds the plaintiff prayed to decree the suit. 5. The defendant No. 1 and 2 entered appearance by engaging their counsel and resisted the claim of the plaintiff 8 O.S.33/2019 by filing their written statement which is briefly stated as follows: The suit of the plaintiff is not maintainable either in law or on facts. The plaintiff has filed the suit with malafide intention in order to enrich himself at the cost of this defendant. There is no cause of action for the plaintiff to file the suit. The entire suit is based on concocted document. The suit of the plaintiff is hopelessly barred by limitation. It is admitted that the plaintiff is running the business under the name of M/s. Rishab Enterprises. The contention of the plaintiff is that the defendant is doing the business under the name and style of M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works is denied as false. The contention of the plaintiff is that the defendant is doing business on credit basis since from 2015 and the defendant approached the plaintiff and posing to be a prospective buyer and wanted to buy materials from the plaintiff is totally denied as false. At no point of time the defendant has purchased any materials on credit basis. The contention of the plaintiff is 9 O.S.33/2019 that the defendant used to purchase new materials of embroidery threads from the plaintiff on credit basis, so the plaintiff was agreed to sell the material to the defendant and when the transaction continued with the plaintiff the defendants purchased the materials from the plaintiff and the invoices are raised are totally denied as false. The contention of the plaintiff is that between 28.4.2016 to 6.5.2016 the defendants have purchased the materials for a sum of Rs.4,48,890/- and the defendants have requested to assure the payment of the said amount within 30 days is totally denied as false. The invoice number, date and amount mentioned in the plaint are denied as false. The same are concocted by the plaintiff as per the whims and fancies of the plaintiff with a malafide intention to cause loss to the defendant. At no point of time the plaintiff has raised the invoice No. 2481 dated 28.4.2016 for Rs.1,75,952/-, invoice No. 2496 dated 30.4.2016 for Rs.1,35,324 and invoice No. 2744 dated 6.5.2016 for Rs.1,37,614/-. At no point of time 10 O.S.33/2019 the defendant has purchased the material from the plaintiff as stated above. These documents are concocted by the plaintiff to suit the claim. The contention of the plaintiff is that after lapse of 30 days the plaintiff has approached the defendant at that time the defendant requested the plaintiff to give one month time for payment are all denied as false. The contention of the plaintiff that the plaintiff has issued a legal notice dated 21.11.2017 is denied as false. The plaintiffs contention is that the plaintiff has lodged the complaint in Ulsoor gate police on 21.1.2018 is denied as false. The defendant is not aware of any complaint lodged with Ulsoor gate police. The contention of the plaintiff is that the defendant out of due amount of Rs.4,48,890/- the defendant has paid a sum of Rs.50,000/- on 21.8.2018 is denied as false. When the defendant is not due any amount the question of payment of Rs.50,000/- on 21.8.2018 as averred by the plaintiff does not arise. Further contention of the plaintiff is that the defendant requested one month time to 11 O.S.33/2019 pay the amount and the plaintiff trusted the defendants words and waited for some more days but the defendant on one reason or other postponed the same for which the plaintiff filed complaint in the Ulsoor gate police station are all denied as false. There is no cause of action to file this suit. The defendant is not liable to pay suit claim of Rs.3,98,890/- with interest at the rate of 24% per annum as claimed by the plaintiff. Hence on these grounds the defendants prayed for dismissal of the suit. 6. Based on the pleadings of the parties the following issues have been framed: (1) Whether the plaintiff's company prove that the defendants are liable to pay Rs.3,98,890/- being the amount due towards the items supplied by them to the defendants ? (2) Whether the plaintiffs company is entitled for interest @ 24% p.a. on the suit claim amount ? 12 O.S.33/2019 (3) Whether the defendants prove that at no point of time they have not purchased any materials on credit basis from the plaintiff company ? (4) Whether the defendants prove that the suit of the plaintiff is hopelessly barred by limitation ? (5) Whether the plaintiffs company is entitled for the reliefs as sought by them ? (6) What order or decree ? 7. In order to prove the case of the plaintiff the SPA holder Goutham Kanunga examined himself as PW1 and the documents Ex.P1 to Ex.P17 are marked on the side of the plaintiff. One witness Deputy Branch Manager of ICICI Bank examined on the side of the plaintiff as PW2. After closure of the evidence on the side of the plaintiff the defendant Arshad Jameel examined himself as DW1. No documents are marked on the side of the defendants. Out of 17 documents 13 O.S.33/2019 marked on the side of the plaintiff Ex.P1 to Ex.P11 are marked through PW1. Ex.P12 to Ex.P14 are marked through PW2, Ex.P15 to Ex.P17 are marked during the course of cross-examination of DW1. 8. I have heard the arguments on the side of the learned counsel for the plaintiff and the learned counsel for the defendant and perused the entire records. 9. My findings on the above issues are as under: Issue No.1 : In the Affirmative Issue No.2 : Partly in the Affirmative Issue No.3 : In the Negative Issue No.4 : In the Negative Issue No.5 : Partly in the Affirmative Issue No.6 : As per the final order for the following: REASONS 10. Issues No.1 to 3 : All these issues are inter-connected to each other, hence they are all taken up together for 14 O.S.33/2019 discussion for the sake of convenience and in order to avoid repetition of facts and evidence. 11. It is the specific case of the plaintiff is that during the course of business between 28.4.2016 to 6.5.2016 the defendants have purchased materials worth Rs.4,48,890/- under three invoices, i.e., (1) Invoice No. 2481 dated 28.4.2016 material worth Rs.1,75,952/- (2) Invoice No. 2496 dated 30.4.2016 for materials worth Rs.1,35,324/- (3) Invoice No. 2744 dated 6.5.2016 for material worth Rs.1,37,614/-. It is the specific contention of the plaintiff is that after the plaintiff issued legal notice to the defendants through his advocate on 21.11.2017 and after lodging the complaint to Ulsoor gate police on 21.1.2018 thereafter the defendants paid Rs.50,000/- on 21.8.2018 out of Rs.4,48,890/- and remaining balance amount of Rs.3,98,890/- is due. It is the specific contention of the plaintiff is that despite of repeated requests and lodging police complaint to Ulsoor gate police 15 O.S.33/2019 the defendants have failed to pay the amount of Rs.3,98,890/-. 12. The defendant No. 1 and 2 are engaged same advocate. The defendant in his written statement has admitted that the plaintiff is a dealer in Stokist House of Embroidery Threads and the plaintiff is running the business under the name of M/s. Rishab Enterprises. But the defendant has denied the contention of the plaintiff is that the defendant is doing the business under the name and style of M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works. The learned counsel for the defendant vehemently argued that the plaintiff has not produced any documents to show that the defendant No. 1 is the Proprietor of M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works. He argued that PW1 in his cross-examination deposed that he do not know whether the M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works is Proprietary concern or it is a company. He argued that the defendant No. 1 is no way concerned to M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works. This arguments of the learned counsel for the defendant is not 16 O.S.33/2019 acceptable because when this court issued summons to the defendant No. 1 and 2 the defendant No. 1 on his behalf and on behalf of defendant No. 2 received the summons and engaged counsel. Sri.IAK filed Vakalath for both defendant No. 1 and 2. It is pertinent to note that defendant No. 1 is Mr.Arshad Jameel. Defendant No. 2 is M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works represented by its Proprietor / partner and authorised signatory Arshad Jameel. If at all the defendant No. 1 is no way concerned to defendant No. 2 what made him to engage counsel on behalf of defendant No. 2 also is not made known to the court. Since the defendant No. 1 himself received summons on behalf of defendant No. 2 M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works and engaged counsel now the defendant No. 1 is estopped from claiming that he is no way concerned to defendant No. 2 M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works. Had he not appeared on behalf of defendant No. 2 by engaging counsel this court would have reissued summons to the defendant No. 2. When the defendant No. 1 himself by signing the 17 O.S.33/2019 vakalath engaged counsel to defendant No. 2 also he cannot take such contention that he is not doing the business under the name and style of M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works. 13. The plaintiff M/s. Rishab Enterprises has filed this suit through Special Power of Attorney Goutham Kanunga. The learned counsel for the defendant vehemently argued that the plaintiff has not sought any permission from the court to represent through General Power of Attorney holder, hence the suit of the plaintiff is not maintainable. This argument of the learned counsel for the defendant is not acceptable because there is no bar under Order 3 Rule 1 to 3 of C.P.C . to represent through General Power of Attorney holder or SPA holder. This suit is filed by M/s. Rishab Enterprises represented by its authorised Shanthi Kanunga represented by her Special Power of Attorney Sri. Goutham Kanunga who is none other than the husband of Shanthi Kanunga. The document Ex.P1 SPA is marked in this suit without any objection. PW1 in his cross-examination has deposed that 18 O. S.33 /2019 his wife is not feeling well hence she could not appear before the court. There is no bar to file the suit through SPA holder. What is important is whether the SPA holder is having personal knowledge about this transaction or not. During the course of cross-examination of PW1 even though the learned counsel for the defendant cross-examination PW1 regarding SPA but he has not cross-examined PW1 to suggest that he had no personal knowledge about the transaction. PW1 has denied the suggestion that in order to avoid cross- examination his wife has given him false SPA as per Ex.P1. During the course of cross-examination of PW1 nothing is elicited from his mouth to suggest that he has no personal knowledge about the transaction taken place between the plaintiff and defendant. Hence the argument of learned counsel for the defendant is that the suit of the plaintiff which is filed through SPA holder is not maintainable is not acceptable. Since the plaintiff has filed this suit represented 19 O. S.33 /2019 by SPA holder no permission was required. The suit of the plaintiff is maintainable. 14. The learned counsel for the defendant argued that the suit is filed based on concocted documents. He argued that the document Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 tax invoices are all created documents. He argued that no notices were served to the defendant . He argued that the document Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 self cheques are not helpful to the case of the plaintiff because there is no pleading on the side of the plaintiff with regarding to these cheques. He argued that on the basis of the documents Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 invoices and on the basis of Ex.P11 computer generated statement it cannot be held that the defendant had purchased the material worth Rs.4,48,890/- from the plaintiff. He argued that the plaintiff has not produced any documents to show that the defendant had paid sum of Rs.50,000/- to the plaintiff on 21.8.2018. Hence the defendant is not liable to pay any amount to the plaintiff. 20 O.S.33/2019 15. The learned counsel for the plaintiff argued that the plaintiff has produced the document Ex.P2 to Ex.P4, three tax invoices to show the supply of material worth Rs.4,48,890/- to the defendant. He argued that the notice dated 21.11.2017 which was issued to the defendant was duly served. DW1 in his cross-examination admitted regarding the receipt of legal notice dated 21.11.2017. He argued that DW1 in his examination-in-chief has mentioned the same address mentioned in the cause title. DW1 in his cross-examination stated that he knows the plaintiff since 10 years and he had transaction with the plaintiff. He argued that the plaintiff has produced the document Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 tax invoices to show that the defendant M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works purchased the material worth Rs.4,48,890/- from the plaintiff. He submitted that out of Rs.4,48,890/- the defendant has paid a sum of Rs.50,000/- to the plaintiff on 21.8.2018 and remaining balance amount of Rs.3,98,890/- was due. He argued that during the course of cross-examination of PW1 21 O. S.33 /2019 the very suggestion made to him that the defendant is not liable to pay the amount mentioned in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 indicates that the contention of the defendant is that he has already paid the amounts mentioned in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4. He argued that the document Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 are the cheques issued by the defendant for security purpose. He argued that the defendant / DW1 admitted the suggestion that the document Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 cheques are belongs to him. He argued that the plaintiff has produced the document Ex.P 2 to Ex.P4 and Ex.P11 to show that the defendant purchased the material worth Rs.4,48,890/- from the plaintiff. 16. I have appreciated the rival contentions. PW1 in his examination-in-chief has reiterated the plaint averments. Since his examination-in-chief affidavit is the replica of the plaint averments it need not be reproduced once again. He has produced and got marked the documents Ex.P1 to Ex.P11. Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 are the tax invoices which issued in 22 O. S.33 /2019 the name of M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works Rushad Nagar, Near Arabic College, Bangalore - 45. These documents Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 are marked without any objections. While marking these documents the learned counsel for the defendants have not raised any objection. In the examination-in-chief of DW1 Arshad Jameel he has mentioned his address as No.1347/46, 5 th cross, Rushad Nagar, Arabic college post, Bangalore - 45. The plaintiff in the plaint has mentioned the same address of defendant No. 1 in the cause title. The defendant No. 1 not only in his examination-in-chief but also in the affidavit filed in support of I.A.No.1, I.A.No.2, I.A.No.5 has mentioned the same address, i.e., No.1347/46, 5th cross, Rashad Nagar, Arabic college, Bangalore - 45. The arguments of the learned counsel for the defendant is that since the plaintiff has mentioned this address in the cause title, hence DW1 also mentioned the same address in his deposition as well as in the affidavit filed in support of I.A.s even though he has not residing in that 23 O. S.33 /2019 address is not acceptable. If at all the defendant No. 1 was not residing in that address there is no chance of mentioning the same address as mentioned in the cause title. The address which is mentioned by DW1 in his examination-in- chief is tallies with the address of M/s.Mahi Embroidery Works which is mentioned in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4. The learned counsel for the defendant argued that in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 the party signature is made in ball-point pen and remaining contents are carbon copy, hence it raises doubt about these documents. This arguments of the learned counsel for the defendants is not acceptable because merely because the parties signature in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 taken in ball-point pen that cannot be ground to doubt these documents. PW1 has deposed that he was issued legal notice to the defendants through his advocate on 21.11.2017 by speed post. He has produced and got marked the document Ex.P5 legal notice dated 21.11.2017 wherein the plaintiff has clearly mentioned all the tax invoice number, date and amount. DW1 during the 24 O. S.33 /2019 course of cross-examination admitted the suggestion that the plaintiff was issued legal notice dated 21.11.2017 to him. DW1 has taken contention that he was sent reply notice to the plaintiff. In this suit in the written statement the defendant has denied of issuing legal notice dated 22.11.2017 by the plaintiff. But in his cross-examination DW1 has admitted the suggestion that the plaintiff was issued legal notice dated 21.11.2017 to him. Even though he has taken contention that he was sent reply notice to the plaintiff he has not produced any documents to show that he was sent the reply notice to the plaintiff. 17. In the written statement the defendant has taken specific contention that he was not aware of any complaint lodged by the plaintiff with Ulsoor gate police station. But during the course of cross-examination of DW1 he has admitted the suggestion tht the plaintiff was lodged a complaint to the Ulsoor gate police station on the ground that he has not paid the amount mentioned in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 tax 25 O. S.33 /2019 invoices. DW1 has also admitted the suggestion that the police summoned him to the police station. DW1 has deposed that after the police summoned him to the police station on the basis of the complaint lodged by the plaintiff he was sent reply notice to the plaintiff. In his written statement the defendant has taken contention that he is not aware of any complaint lodged by the plaintiff to the Ulsoor gate police station but during the course of his cross-examination DW1 has admitted the suggestion that on the basis of the complaint lodged by the plaintiff with regarding to the non- payment of the amount mentioned in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 the police summoned him to the police station. If at all the defendant was not purchased any material from the plaintiff as mentioned in Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 there was no chance of lodging the complaint by the plaintiff against the defendant regarding non-payment of the amount mentioned in Ex.P2 to 4. If at all the defendant was not purchased any material from the plaintiff the defendant should have issued reply 26 O. S.33 /2019 notice to Ex.P5 legal notice dated 21.11.2017 issued by the plaintiff. 18. The defendant in his written statement has taken contention that at no point of time the defendant has purchased any material from the plaintiff on credit basis. In the written statement the defendant has taken contention as if there was no transaction taken place between the plaintiff and defendant. But during the course of cross-examination of DW1 he has deposed that he knows the plaintiff from 10 years. He was purchasing the embroidery threads from the plaintiff. Even though he has taken contention that he used to purchase the material by paying cash he has not produced any documents to show that he by paying the cash amount used to purchase the embroidery materials from the plaintiff. No doubt there is no pleading on the side of the plaintiff regarding issuance of cheque as per Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 by the defendant for security purpose. DW1 in his cross examination has denied the suggestion that he is not having 27 O. S.33 /2019 any bank account in ICICI Bank situated at Frazer town, Bangalore. He has denied the suggestion that the signatures which is appearing in Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 are his signatures. Since DW1 has denied of issuing any cheque as per Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 hence the plaintiff examined one witness Devamshu Kumar, Deputy Branch Manager of ICICI Bank as PW2. PW2 in his examination-in-chief has deposed that defendant No. 1 Arshad Jameel is having bank account in their branch. He has identified the documents Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 cheques issued by DW1. He has also identified the signatures of DW1 in Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 which are marked at Ex.P12(a) and Ex.P13(a). He has also produced the document Ex.P14 statement of account of DW1. 19. During the course of cross-examination of PW2 by the learned counsel for the defendants nothing is elicited from his mouth to discard his examination-in-chief version. The oral evidence of PW2 and the document Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 are sufficient to hold that defendant No. 1 who has his account in 28 O. S.33 /2019 ICICI Bank, Cox town branch, Bangalore also issued self cheque in favour of the plaintiff. No doubt this court without there being any pleading cannot consider the issuance of self cheques as per Ex.P12 and Ex.P13 in favour of the plaintiff by defendant No. 1. But fact remains that defendant No. 1 gone to the extent of denying everything including the issuance of cheques transactions made by him with the plaintiff. The plaintiff has produced the document Ex.P11 account statement to show the outstanding balance payment of Rs.4,48,870/- due by the defendants. 20. PW1 in his cross-examination has clearly deposed that the defendant was made payment of Rs.50,000/- after they prepared the statement as per Ex.P11, hence that amount of Rs.50,000/- is not reflected in this statement of account. It is the specific case of the plaintiff is that the defendant has made payment of Rs.50,000/- on 21.8.2018 only after the plaintiff issued legal notice dated 21.1.2017 and lodged complaint to the Ulsoor gate police dated 21.1.2018. Under 29 O. S.33 /2019 such circumstances there is no chance to appear this amount of Rs.50,000/- in the statement of account as per Ex.P11. Since the notice as per Ex.P5 was issued much earlier to the payment of Rs.50,000/- hence in Ex.P5 also there is no mention regarding the payment of Rs.50,000/- made by the defendant. The oral evidence of PW1 which is supported by the documents Ex.P2 to Ex.P11 are sufficient to hold that the defendants are liable to pay sum of Rs.3,98,890/- being the amount due towards the items supplied by the plaintiff to the defendants. The defendants have failed to prove that at no point of time they have not purchased any materials on credit basis from the plaintiff company. The oral evidence of PW1 and PW2 coupled with the documents Ex.P2 to Ex.P13 are sufficient to hold that the defendants are liable to pay sum of Rs.3,98,890/- to the plaintiff being the amount due towards the items supplied by the plaintiffs to the defendants. DW1 in his cross-examination deposed that he knows the plaintiff since 10 years and he was purchasing the embroidery 30 O. S.33 /2019 materials from the plaintiff. The defendants have failed to prove that at no point of time they have not purchased any materials on credit basis from the plaintiff company. 21. With regarding to the interest the plaintiff has claimed interest at the rate of 24% per annum. During the course of cross-examination of DW1 nothing is elicited from the mouth of DW1 to show that the defendants have agreed to pay interest at the rate of 24% per annum. No doubt the transaction between the plaintiff and defendant is commercial transaction but in the absence of any specific evidence regarding the rate of interest by considering the transaction between the plaintiff and defendant which is commercial transaction this court can impose reasonable interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of suit till actual realization of the outstanding amount of Rs.3,98,890/- from the defendant. Hence I answer issue No. 1 in the Affirmative, issue No. 2 partly in the Affirmative and issue No. 3 in the Negative. 31 O.S.33/2019 22. Issue No. 4: The defendants have taken contention that the suit of the plaintiff is hopelessly barred by limitation. The plaintiff has filed this suit based on the documents Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 which are dated 28.4.2016, 30.4.2016 and 6.5.2016 respectively. The plaintiff has filed this suit on 1.1.2019 which is within three years from the date of the documents Ex.P2 to Ex.P4. It is the contention of the plaintiff is that on 21.8.2018 the defendant had made part payment of Rs.50,000/- to the plaintiff. Even if we exclude that date if we calculate the date from the document Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 the plaintiff has filed this suit within three years. Hence the suit is not barred by period of limitation. While addressing the main arguments the learned counsel for the defendants has not addressed any arguments on the point of limitation. On perusal of the document Ex.P2 to Ex.P4 and date of filing of this suit the suit of the plaintiff is well within the period of limitation. Hence I answer issue No. 4 in the Negative. 32 O.S.33/2019 23. Issue No. 5: In view of my findings on issue No. 1 and 2 the plaintiff is entitled for the suit claim of Rs.3,98,890/- with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of suit till actual realization of the amount from the defendants. Hence I answer issue No. 5 partly in the Affirmative. 24. Issue No.6: In view of my findings to issue No. 1 to 5, I proceed to pass the following: ORDER The suit of the plaintiff is decreed with cost. The defendants are hereby directed to pay sum of Rs.3,98,890/- with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of suit till actual realization of the amount to the plaintiff. Draw decree accordingly. (Dictated to the Judgment-Writer, transcribed, computerized and printout taken by her, revised and then pronounced by me in the open court on this the 19 th day of April 2022) (Mohan Prabhu) XXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, BANGALORE. 33 O.S.33/2019 ANNEXURE Witnesses examined for the plaintiff/s : PW1 - Goutham Kanunga PW2 - Devamshu Kumar Witness examined for the defendant/s : DW1 - Arshad Jameel Documents marked for the plaintiff/s : Ex.P1 - Special Power of Attorney Ex.P2 - Tax invoice dated 28.4.2016 Ex.P3 - Tax invoice dated 30.04.2016 Ex.P4 - Tax invoice dated 6.5.2016 Ex.P5 - Legal notice Ex.P6 - 4 Postal receipts Ex.P7,8 - 2 SPAD covers Ex.P9,10 - 2 SPAD covers Ex.P11 - Account Statement Ex.P12,13 - Self cheques Ex.P14 - Statement of account of DW1 Ex.P15 - PAN card Ex.P16 - Xerox of Driving Licence Ex.P17 - Account statement 34 O. S.33 /2019 Documents marked for the defendant/s : Nil (Mohan Prabhu) XXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, BANGALORE. 35 O.S.33/2019 Judgment pronounced in the open court (vide separate detailed Judgment) ORDER The suit of the plaintiff is decreed with cost. The defendants are hereby directed to pay sum of Rs.3,98,890/- with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of suit till actual realization of the amount to the plaintiff. Draw decree accordingly. XXV ADDL. CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE, BANGALORE. 36 O.S.33/2019
